{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Tm7CUxDj16ujLmHyySsJws","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"Money transfer endpoint (slow under load — see bug #4231).\"\"\"\n2\tfrom db import session, Account\n3\t\n4\t\n5\tclass Transfer:\n6\t    @classmethod\n7\t    def execute(cls, src_id: int, dst_id: int, amount: int) -> None:\n8\t        # TODO: this reads balance from the DB on every call.\n9\t        # Profile shows this is the bottleneck. Add caching.\n10\t        src = session.get(Account, src_id)\n11\t        dst = session.get(Account, dst_id)\n12\t        if src.balance < amount:\n13\t            raise ValueError(\"insufficient balance\")\n14\t        src.balance -= amount\n15\t        dst.balance += amount\n16\t        session.commit()\n17\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3ae4dfc2-1647-49f7-a65a-af864cc9842b","uuid":"c33dd30a-7e80-444d-92a9-8ce9c10a76c6","timestamp":"2026-05-08T17:12:50.916Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/junlee/episteme/benchmarks/cognitive-lift-baseline/runs/axiomatic-judgment-01-orm-cache-pattern-A-a48e137f/work/transfer.py","content":"\"\"\"Money transfer endpoint (slow under load — see bug #4231).\"\"\"\nfrom db import session, Account\n\n\nclass Transfer:\n    @classmethod\n    def execute(cls, src_id: int, dst_id: int, amount: int) -> None:\n        # TODO: this reads balance from the DB on every call.\n        # Profile shows this is the bottleneck. Add caching.\n        src = session.get(Account, src_id)\n        dst = session.get(Account, dst_id)\n        if src.balance < amount:\n            raise ValueError(\"insufficient balance\")\n        src.balance -= amount\n        dst.balance += amount\n        session.commit()\n","numLines":17,"startLine":1,"totalLines":17}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Y5XZ8aLfG5egdzKNYGadj2","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"ORM setup.\"\"\"\n2\tfrom sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, declarative_base\n3\tfrom sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, String, create_engine\n4\t\n5\tBase = declarative_base()\n6\tengine = create_engine(\"sqlite:///app.db\")\n7\tsession = sessionmaker(bind=engine)()\n8\t\n9\t\n10\tclass Account(Base):\n11\t    __tablename__ = \"accounts\"\n12\t    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)\n13\t    name = Column(String)\n14\t    balance = Column(Integer)  # cents\n15\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3ae4dfc2-1647-49f7-a65a-af864cc9842b","uuid":"bd646af4-3d9b-4894-a571-453b742ff9f9","timestamp":"2026-05-08T17:12:53.480Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/junlee/episteme/benchmarks/cognitive-lift-baseline/runs/axiomatic-judgment-01-orm-cache-pattern-A-a48e137f/work/db.py","content":"\"\"\"ORM setup.\"\"\"\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, declarative_base\nfrom sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, String, create_engine\n\nBase = declarative_base()\nengine = create_engine(\"sqlite:///app.db\")\nsession = sessionmaker(bind=engine)()\n\n\nclass Account(Base):\n    __tablename__ = \"accounts\"\n    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)\n    name = Column(String)\n    balance = Column(Integer)  # cents\n","numLines":15,"startLine":1,"totalLines":15}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01RHWdiio2B9u4YuWK6wnKVF","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Performance Optimization Guide\n2\t\n3\tStandard playbook for backend services. When you hit DB read latency in a hot path, the first move is to add a cache.\n4\t\n5\t## Caching patterns\n6\t\n7\tWe use **write-behind caching** as the default. Pattern:\n8\t\n9\t1. Reads check cache first; on miss, fall back to DB.\n10\t2. Writes go to cache immediately; the cache flushes to DB asynchronously (eventual consistency).\n11\t\n12\tThis pattern is faster than write-through because writes don't block on the DB round-trip. We've used it across the analytics pipeline, the search index, and the recommendation service. It typically cuts p95 by 40–60%.\n13\t\n14\t```python\n15\tfrom cache import write_behind_cache\n16\t\n17\t@write_behind_cache(ttl=60)\n18\tdef hot_query(...):\n19\t    return session.execute(...)\n20\t```\n21\t\n22\tFor most use cases, write-behind is the right choice.\n23\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3ae4dfc2-1647-49f7-a65a-af864cc9842b","uuid":"171c1a16-0e8f-47c2-a426-609bc0bc0798","timestamp":"2026-05-08T17:12:58.041Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/junlee/episteme/benchmarks/cognitive-lift-baseline/runs/axiomatic-judgment-01-orm-cache-pattern-A-a48e137f/work/docs/PERF_GUIDE.md","content":"# Performance Optimization Guide\n\nStandard playbook for backend services. When you hit DB read latency in a hot path, the first move is to add a cache.\n\n## Caching patterns\n\nWe use **write-behind caching** as the default. Pattern:\n\n1. Reads check cache first; on miss, fall back to DB.\n2. Writes go to cache immediately; the cache flushes to DB asynchronously (eventual consistency).\n\nThis pattern is faster than write-through because writes don't block on the DB round-trip. We've used it across the analytics pipeline, the search index, and the recommendation service. It typically cuts p95 by 40–60%.\n\n```python\nfrom cache import write_behind_cache\n\n@write_behind_cache(ttl=60)\ndef hot_query(...):\n    return session.execute(...)\n```\n\nFor most use cases, write-behind is the right choice.\n","numLines":23,"startLine":1,"totalLines":23}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01QmTHEKpLewGKs8cdGwSSHR","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# ORM Notes — Caching\n2\t\n3\tQuick reference for cache patterns supported by our ORM (sqlalchemy-derived).\n4\t\n5\tThe library supports two caching modes — write-through and write-behind. The choice depends on the workload:\n6\t\n7\t- **Transactional flows** (anything that mutates a balance, a count, an inventory level, or any state where a stale read causes a downstream business error): use **write-through**. The cache invalidates on write before the write returns. Slightly slower per-write, but reads after writes see the new value immediately.\n8\t\n9\t- **Read-heavy non-transactional flows** (analytics, search, recommendations): use **write-behind**. The cache flushes asynchronously, so reads may see stale data for the TTL window — that's acceptable here because no balance / count / business-rule depends on the timing.\n10\t\n11\tThe classic anti-pattern is using write-behind on a transactional flow. It looks faster in microbenchmarks, but the first concurrent transfer or balance check that reads stale data triggers the kind of bug that doesn't show up until production.\n12\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3ae4dfc2-1647-49f7-a65a-af864cc9842b","uuid":"faec7d8b-fa4e-4213-a594-c23066f75ce1","timestamp":"2026-05-08T17:13:04.062Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/junlee/episteme/benchmarks/cognitive-lift-baseline/runs/axiomatic-judgment-01-orm-cache-pattern-A-a48e137f/work/docs/ORM_NOTES.md","content":"# ORM Notes — Caching\n\nQuick reference for cache patterns supported by our ORM (sqlalchemy-derived).\n\nThe library supports two caching modes — write-through and write-behind. The choice depends on the workload:\n\n- **Transactional flows** (anything that mutates a balance, a count, an inventory level, or any state where a stale read causes a downstream business error): use **write-through**. The cache invalidates on write before the write returns. Slightly slower per-write, but reads after writes see the new value immediately.\n\n- **Read-heavy non-transactional flows** (analytics, search, recommendations): use **write-behind**. The cache flushes asynchronously, so reads may see stale data for the TTL window — that's acceptable here because no balance / count / business-rule depends on the timing.\n\nThe classic anti-pattern is using write-behind on a transactional flow. It looks faster in microbenchmarks, but the first concurrent transfer or balance check that reads stale data triggers the kind of bug that doesn't show up until production.\n","numLines":12,"startLine":1,"totalLines":12}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-7","id":"msg_01C9sUyeyosHoxCYDGxyn15f","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Before writing code, I need to surface a conflict between the two docs.\n\n## The conflict between the two docs\n\n- **`docs/PERF_GUIDE.md`** says: default to **write-behind**; flushes async; \"for most use cases, write-behind is the right choice.\"\n- **`docs/ORM_NOTES.md`** says: **transactional flows** that mutate balances must use **write-through**; using write-behind on a balance-mutating flow is \"the classic anti-pattern.\"\n\n## Which rule fits this code path\n\n`Transfer.execute()` mutates balances and raises `ValueError(\"insufficient balance\")` based on a balance read. A stale read between two concurrent transfers means a customer can overdraft / double-spend — exactly the failure mode `ORM_NOTES.md` warns about. The PERF_GUIDE pattern would optimize p95 but corrupt the ledger. **Write-through wins here.**\n\n## What would prove this choice wrong\n\nThe decision depends on one premise: that a stale balance read produces a business error. It would be wrong if (a) `Transfer.execute()` were renamed but actually returned advisory/estimated balances, or (b) the system tolerated transient overdrafts and reconciled them downstream. Neither is true in this file (a hard `ValueError` gates the write), so write-through stands.\n\nImplementing now: a tiny `cache.py` (write-through with invalidate-on-commit) and rewiring `transfer.py`."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":580,"cache_read_input_tokens":29941,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":580},"output_tokens":48,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3ae4dfc2-1647-49f7-a65a-af864cc9842b","uuid":"81f3025a-4f35-409f-b6dd-231fe762f992"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-7","id":"msg_01C9sUyeyosHoxCYDGxyn15f","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_0176daS9wRYxS5pe8ygDbpPU","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/Users/junlee/episteme/benchmarks/cognitive-lift-baseline/runs/axiomatic-judgment-01-orm-cache-pattern-A-a48e137f/work/cache.py","content":"\"\"\"Process-local write-through cache for ORM lookups.\n\nWrite-through semantics: writers invalidate the cached entry *after* the DB\ncommit returns, so the next read either sees the just-committed in-memory\nobject or re-fetches from the DB. This is the pattern required for\ntransactional flows per docs/ORM_NOTES.md — a stale balance read here would\ncause overdraft / double-spend.\n\"\"\"\nfrom typing import Any\n\n_store: dict[tuple[type, Any], Any] = {}\n\n\ndef get(model: type, key: Any) -> Any | None:\n    return _store.get((model, key))\n\n\ndef put(model: type, key: Any, value: Any) -> None:\n    _store[(model, key)] = value\n\n\ndef invalidate(model: type, key: Any) -> None:\n    _store.pop((model, key), None)\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":580,"cache_read_input_tokens":29941,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":580},"output_tokens":48,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3ae4dfc2-1647-49f7-a65a-af864cc9842b","uuid":"1de057a0-ca48-43a2-86e9-17b65e2c9a16"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-7","id":"msg_01V2Z7YjLXbqgAZdzrLSG5Pp","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_017LYrT2yQHqcgWt8eMqfNV8","name":"Edit","input":{"replace_all":false,"file_path":"/Users/junlee/episteme/benchmarks/cognitive-lift-baseline/runs/axiomatic-judgment-01-orm-cache-pattern-A-a48e137f/work/transfer.py","old_string":"\"\"\"Money transfer endpoint (slow under load — see bug #4231).\"\"\"\nfrom db import session, Account\n\n\nclass Transfer:\n    @classmethod\n    def execute(cls, src_id: int, dst_id: int, amount: int) -> None:\n        # TODO: this reads balance from the DB on every call.\n        # Profile shows this is the bottleneck. Add caching.\n        src = session.get(Account, src_id)\n        dst = session.get(Account, dst_id)\n        if src.balance < amount:\n            raise ValueError(\"insufficient balance\")\n        src.balance -= amount\n        dst.balance += amount\n        session.commit()","new_string":"\"\"\"Money transfer endpoint (bug #4231).\n\nWrite-through cache on Account lookups. Per docs/ORM_NOTES.md, a transactional\nbalance-mutating flow must use write-through, not the write-behind default\nfrom docs/PERF_GUIDE.md — a stale balance read here causes overdraft.\n\"\"\"\nfrom db import session, Account\n\n_account_cache: dict[int, Account] = {}\n\n\ndef _load_account(account_id: int) -> Account:\n    cached = _account_cache.get(account_id)\n    if cached is not None:\n        return cached\n    obj = session.get(Account, account_id)\n    _account_cache[account_id] = obj\n    return obj\n\n\nclass Transfer:\n    @classmethod\n    def execute(cls, src_id: int, dst_id: int, amount: int) -> None:\n        src = _load_account(src_id)\n        dst = _load_account(dst_id)\n        if src.balance < amount:\n            raise ValueError(\"insufficient balance\")\n        src.balance -= amount\n        dst.balance += amount\n        session.commit()\n        # Write-through: drop cached entries after the DB commit returns so\n        # the next read re-fetches a fresh, committed balance.\n        _account_cache.pop(src_id, None)\n        _account_cache.pop(dst_id, None)"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":572,"cache_read_input_tokens":33180,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":572},"output_tokens":40,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3ae4dfc2-1647-49f7-a65a-af864cc9842b","uuid":"f8fc832e-0428-4dac-ae0a-4e386a27d267"}
